_doc: ★8 B-tier ITP — a NON-INDUCTIVE invariant (the classic loop bug). The invariant `sum <= i` is TRUE on entry but the body adds 2 to sum while i grows by 1, so it is NOT preserved. The init VC passes; the STEP VC fails and Z3 returns a concrete pre-state where one iteration breaks the invariant. This is generate-and-check working: the tool refuses to call a bad invariant proved.
name: leaky-accumulator

_expected.step: REFUTED   — sum<=i ∧ sum'=sum+2 ∧ i'=i+1  ⊭  sum'<=i'  (counterexample e.g. i=0, sum=0 ⇒ 2 <= 1 false)
_expected.verdict: NOT proved — the step VC carries the counterexample
